---
title: "FunctionalKPg"
output: html_document
date: "2026-01-23"
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = FALSE)
```

```{R}
#install necessary packages
if (!require(rstudioapi)){install.packages("rstudioapi")}
me <- (rstudioapi::getActiveDocumentContext()$path) #get current file name
wd <- dirname(me)

if (!require(renv)){ install.packages("renv")}

#because I use an old version of iNEXT
if(!require(iNEXT)){remotes::install_version("iNEXT", version = "2.0.20")}

packs <- unique(renv::dependencies(me)$Package) #list of packages needed 

for (i in seq_along(packs)){
  if (!require(packs[i], character.only=T)){ #install any missing pacls
    install.packages(packs[i])
  }
}

lapply(packs, library, character.only=T) #load packages

rm(me, packs, i) #clean env

```


## Import Data

```{r}
data <- read.csv(paste0(wd, "/Data/RZN_TCC_CO_2026.csv"))
#names of columns to choose
names(data)
#pick relevant columns to add to working data
wk_data <- data[,c(5,6,12,13)] #meter, Section, d13C, d18O

#rename columns to make it easier to see whats happening (new_name = old_name)
library(dplyr)
wk_data <- wk_data %>% rename(
  "d13C" = "d.13C.12C..VPDB",
  "d18O" = "d.18O.16O..VPDB"
)

#add column to working data that indicates the data is mine
wk_data$Origin <- "Nolan"
#add column for more details about location
wk_data$Country <- "USA"
#add lat and lng
#wk_data$
```

```{r}
#data from published sources
#cols to match:
names(wk_data)

#Sosa-Montes de Oca et al., 2024 via OSF
tcc_SMdO24 <- read.csv(paste0(wd, "/Data/SMdO_TCC_2024.csv"))
SMdO <- tcc_SMdO24[,c("meter", "d13C_VPDB", "d18O_VPDB")]
SMdO <- SMdO %>% rename( "d13C" = "d13C_VPDB", "d18O" = "d18O_VPDB")
SMdO$Section <- "TCC-F"
SMdO$Origin <- "Sosa-Montes_de_Oca"
SMdO$Country <- "USA"
wk_data <- rbind(wk_data, SMdO)

#Sosa-Montes de Oca, C. (2024). Intense changes in the main source of organic carbon to the ocean following the Cretaceous-Paleogene boundary (dataset). https://doi 10.17605/OSF.IO/ZMA9G

#Jones etal 2023 - El Kef
elkef_J23 <- read.csv(paste0(wd, "/Data/Jones_etal_2023_ElKef.csv"))
#Jones, Heather L.; al., et (2022). Supplemental Material: Stratigraphy of the Cretaceous/Paleogene (K/Pg) boundary at the Global Stratotype Section and Point (GSSP) in El Kef, Tunisia: New insights from the El Kef Coring Project. Geological Society of America. Journal contribution. https://doi.org/10.1130/GSAB.S.21445755.v1

#El Kef Data Notes
#Hole A - KPg is at core 16
#Holes B and C are further up in Danian
#Holes D and D have KPg at core 10

#Sepulveda et al 2019 - 
euro_S19 <- read.csv(paste0(wd, "/Data/Sepulveda_etal_2019.csv"))
#Sepúlveda, Julio; Alegret, Laia; Haddad, Emily; Cao, Changqun; Summons, Roger E; Thomas, Ellen (2019): Bulk elemental and stable isotope data across the Cretaceous-Paleogene boundary [dataset]. PANGAEA, https://doi.org/10.1594/PANGAEA.902759, Supplement to: Sepúlveda, Julio; Alegret, Laia; Thomas, Ellen; Haddad, Emily; Cao, Changqun; Summons, Roger E (2019): Stable Isotope Constraints on Marine Productivity Across the Cretaceous-Paleogene Mass Extinction. Paleoceanography and Paleoclimatology, 34(7), 1195-1217, https://doi.org/10.1029/2018PA003442

#Divided into cores from 4 different countries - 
unique(euro_S19$Event)
unique(euro_S19$Location)

#Hull et al 2020 Impact Volcanism

#     Pincelli M. Hull et al. On impact and volcanism across the Cretaceous-Paleogene boundary.Science367,266-272(2020).DOI:10.1126/science.aay5055
odp_H20_f <- read.csv(paste0(wd, "/Data/Hull_d18O_foram.csv"))
odp_H20_b <- read.csv(paste0(wd, "/Data/Hull_d18O_bulk.csv"))
odp_H20_s <- read.csv(paste0(wd, "/Data/Hull_d18O_shell.csv"))

#Alegret et al 2012 - d18O used by Hull et al 2020, here is corr d13C data
odp_A12 <- read.csv(paste0(wd, "/Data/Alegret_etal_2012_C.csv"))
#http://www.pnas.org/lookup/suppl/doi:10.1073/pnas.1110601109/-/DCSupplemental/pnas.1110601109_SI.pdf?targetid=STXT
#L. Alegret, E. Thomas, & K.C. Lohmann, End-Cretaceous marine mass extinction not caused by productivity collapse, Proc. Natl. Acad. Sci. U.S.A. 109 (3) 728-732, https://doi.org/10.1073/pnas.1110601109 (2012).

```

```{r}
#clean up environment
try(
  rm(
    SMdO, tcc_SMdO24,
    
  )
)
```

## Separate into strat sections and plot by section

```{r}
#make separate dataset for each section
sect_data <- list()
sections <- unique(wk_data$Section)

for (i in seq_along(sections)){
  name <- sections[[i]]
  dat <- filter(wk_data, Section == name)
  sect_data[[name]] <- dat
}

rm(i, dat, name)
```

```{r, eval=FALSE}
#plot each section and compile them all into a figure
library(ggplot2)
plots <- list()

for (i in seq_along(sect_data)){
  dat <- sect_data[[i]]
  name <- paste0(dat$Section[1]) #give it the name of the section
  
  d13C <- dat$d.13C.12C..VPDB
  meter <- dat$meter
  
  p <- ggplot(data = dat, aes(x = meter, y = d13C)) +
    theme_classic() +
    geom_point(color = "#8d58b8", shape = 2) +
    coord_flip() +
    geom_smooth(color = "#463c82", fill = "#c1bae8") +
    labs(y = "d13C", x = "meter", title = name)
  
  plots[[name]] <- p
    
}

```

```{r}
#d13C plots
dat <- wk_data
d13C <- dat$d.13C.12C..VPDB
d18O <- dat$d.18O.16O..VPDB
meter <- dat$meter

p_d13C <- ggplot(data = dat, aes(x = meter, y = d13C)) +
    theme_bw() +
    geom_point(color = "#4b8c3c", shape = 2) + #add point data
    ylim((min(d13C)-0.01), (max(d13C)+0.01)) + #set axis limits to just larger than data values
  
    coord_flip() + #flip x and y so that meter is vertical
  
    geom_smooth(color = "#17400d", fill = "#aecca7") + #add smoothed loess fit
  

  
    geom_hline(aes(yintercept = 0)) + #add vertical line at d13C = 0
  
    geom_vline(aes(xintercept = 0), color = "#994b6b") + #add horizontal line at K-Pg boundary
  
    labs(y = "d13C", 
         x = "meter", 
         title = "d13C Plots",
         caption = "K-Pg Boundary at 0 m") + #add axis labels
  
    facet_grid(. ~ Section)  #break up into section plots
    
    

plot(p_d13C)

setwd(paste0(wd, "/Figs"))
ggsave("p_d13C.png", width = 10, height = 6)
setwd(wd)

#plot d18O
p_d18O <- ggplot(data = dat, aes(x = meter, y = d18O)) +
    theme_bw() +
    geom_point(color = "#346aa3", shape = 2) + #add point data
    ylim((min(d18O)-0.01), (max(d18O)+0.01)) + #set axis limits to just larger than data values
  
    coord_flip() + #flip x and y so that meter is vertical
  
    geom_smooth(color = "#103a66", fill = "#b1c7de") + #add smoothed loess fit
  

  
    geom_hline(aes(yintercept = 0)) + #add vertical line at d13C = 0
  
    geom_vline(aes(xintercept = 0), color = "#994b6b") + #add horizontal line at K-Pg boundary
  
    labs(y = "d18O", 
         x = "meter", 
         title = "d18O Plots",
         caption = "K-Pg Boundary at 0 m") + #add axis labels
  
    facet_grid(. ~ Section)  #break up into section plots
    
    

plot(p_d18O)


setwd(paste0(wd, "/Figs"))
ggsave("p_d18O.png", width = 10, height = 6)
setwd(wd)
```

# Temperatures from d18O 
```{r}
#calculate temperatures given d18O data
library(isogeochem)

#use function isogeochem::temp_d18O(d18O_c_VSMOW, d18O_H2O_VSMOW, min, eq))
#d18O_c_VSMOW	= Carbonate d18O value expressed on the VSMOW scale (‰).
#d18O_H2O_VSMOW	= Water d18O value expressed on the VSMOW scale (‰).
#min = mineralogy; Options are "calcite", "aragonite", "apatite", "siderite", and "dolomite".
#eq = "ONeil69"

#add column to data with VSMOW d18O (from VPDB)
wk_data$d18O_VSMOW <- to_VSMOW(wk_data$d.18O.16O..VPDB)
H2O_VSMOW <- to_VSMOW(0)

#add column calculating temps (deg C)
wk_data$Temp_0 <- temp_d18O(wk_data$d18O_VSMOW, 0, "calcite", "ONeil69")
wk_data$Temp_min1 <- temp_d18O(wk_data$d18O_VSMOW, -1, "calcite", "ONeil69")
```

##Plot Temperature Data
```{r}
#plot Temp given water d18O value = 0 (VSMOW)
library(ggplot2)
p_Temp_0 <- ggplot(data = wk_data, aes(x = meter, y = Temp_0)) +
    theme_bw() +
    geom_point(color = "#8d58b8", shape = 2) + #add point data
    ylim((min(wk_data$Temp_0)-0.01), (max(wk_data$Temp_0)+0.01)) + #set axis limits to just larger than data values
  
    coord_flip() + #flip x and y so that meter is vertical
  
    geom_smooth(color = "#463c82", fill = "#c1bae8") + #add smoothed loess fit
  

  
    geom_hline(aes(yintercept = 0)) + #add vertical line at d13C = 0
  
    geom_vline(aes(xintercept = 0), color = "#994b6b") + #add horizontal line at K-Pg boundary
  
    labs(y = "Temperature (deg C)", 
         x = "meter", 
         title = "Temperature Plots",
         caption = "K-Pg Boundary at 0 m") + #add axis labels
  
    facet_grid(. ~ Section)  #break up into section plots
    
plot(p_Temp_0)


setwd(paste0(wd, "/Figs"))
ggsave("p_Temp_0.png", width = 10, height = 6)
setwd(wd)

#plot Temperature (given water d18O value = -1 (VSMOW))
library(ggplot2)
p_Temp_min1 <- ggplot(data = wk_data, aes(x = meter, y = Temp_min1)) +
    theme_bw() +
    geom_point(color = "#8d58b8", shape = 2) + #add point data
    ylim((min(wk_data$Temp_min1)-0.01), (max(wk_data$Temp_min1)+0.01)) + #set axis limits to just larger than data values
  
    coord_flip() + #flip x and y so that meter is vertical
  
    geom_smooth(color = "#463c82", fill = "#c1bae8") + #add smoothed loess fit
  

  
    geom_hline(aes(yintercept = 0)) + #add vertical line at d13C = 0
  
    geom_vline(aes(xintercept = 0), color = "#994b6b") + #add horizontal line at K-Pg boundary
  
    labs(y = "Temperature (deg C)", 
         x = "meter", 
         title = "Temperature Plots",
         caption = "K-Pg Boundary at 0 m") + #add axis labels
  
    facet_grid(. ~ Section)  #break up into section plots
    
    

plot(p_Temp_min1)


setwd(paste0(wd, "/Figs"))
ggsave("p_Temp_min1.png", width = 10, height = 6)
setwd(wd)
```
```{r}
#plot d13C and d18O together
p_CO <- ggplot(data = wk_data) +
    theme_bw() +
  #plot d13C
    geom_point(aes(x = meter, y = d13C), color = "#4b8c3c", shape = 2) + #add point data
    #geom_smooth(aes(x = meter, y = d13C), color = "#17400d", fill = "#aecca7") + #add smoothed loess fit
  
  #plot d18O
    geom_point(aes(x = meter, y = d18O), color = "#346aa3", shape = 2) + #add point data
    #geom_smooth(aes(x = meter, y = d18O), color = "#103a66", fill = "#b1c7de") + #add smoothed loess fit
  
    coord_flip() + #flip x and y so that meter is vertical

    geom_hline(aes(yintercept = 0)) + #add vertical line at d13C = 0
  
    geom_vline(aes(xintercept = 0), color = "#994b6b") + #add horizontal line at K-Pg boundary
  
    labs(y = "per mil", 
         x = "meter", 
         title = "d13C and d18OPlots",
         caption = "K-Pg Boundary at 0 m") + #add axis labels
  
    facet_grid(. ~ Section)  #break up into section plots

plot(p_CO)
```
